title: Symfony ExpressionLanguage — canonical examples
description: Sample expressions covering common approval-flow patterns. Consumed by the template-authoring UI's 'starter templates' picker + integration tests. Every expression parses via ExpressionLanguageAdapter without error + evaluates against the sample context to the given expected result.

when_expressions.purpose: Refunds over $500 need approval; smaller refunds pass through.
when_expressions.notes: Numeric comparison. Empty when_expression = always matches.

when_expressions.purpose: High-value refunds initiated by non-manager users
Weekend + after-hours privileged actions require additional approval

selector_expressions.purpose: Every finance manager in the tenant (delegation-aware)
selector_expressions.notes: Basic set — available in all tiers. Delegation substitution happens automatically.

selector_expressions.purpose: Every user holding a specific permission
Owner of the resource being acted upon
selector_expressions.notes: Resolves via the resource's created_by column.

selector_expressions.purpose: Union — security team OR the resource owner
Intersection — must be BOTH a finance manager AND in the requester's branch

selector_expressions.notes: except takes a user_id string or an array of user_ids.

selector_expressions.purpose: Direct manager of the requester (enterprise tier)
selector_expressions.notes: Requires approval_advanced_expressions entitlement. Resolves via user_metadata.manager_id.

selector_expressions.purpose: Chain upward to the first CFO in the org tree (enterprise tier — deferred to Wave 2)
selector_expressions.notes: Walk from requester upward through user_metadata.manager_id chain, stop at first user matching selector.

selector_expressions.purpose: Every tenant admin except OOF-delegating ones (avoid double-hop)

compilation_notes: Every expression is compiled to an AST once + cached in Redis keyed on SHA-256 of the source. Cache TTL 60s.
Runtime errors + parse errors + wall-clock timeouts (250ms hard deadline) all fire ApproverSelectorEvaluationFailed events + refuse the parent operation.

The grammar is CLOSED — no dynamic PHP method calls, no user-defined function loading. Only the whitelisted selector functions (`role`, `user`, `permission`, `owner_of`, `manager_of`, `any`, `all`, `except`, `chain_up_to`) are available.
Available variables in when_expression: action_key, subject_type, subject_id, context (spread as top-level too), requester (User), tenant (Tenant), now (DateTime), today (Date).
Available variables in selector_expression: same as when_expression PLUS `resource` (the polymorphic subject shorthand).
